Apple___Apple_scab: Remove and destroy infected leaves. Use appropriate fungicides like Captan or Myclobutanil.
Apple___healthy: Your apple leaf is healthy. Continue regular pruning and monitoring.
Banana___Black_Sigatoka: 1. Remove and burn infected leaves. 2. Apply fungicides (Mancozeb or Chlorothalonil). 3. Improve air circulation by thinning plants. 4. Avoid overhead irrigation.
Banana___healthy: Your banana leaf is healthy. Maintain consistent watering and fertilization.
Corn___Common_rust: Apply fungicides containing azoxystrobin or pyraclostrobin when spots first appear. Consider rust-resistant hybrids for next season.
Corn___healthy: Your corn leaf is healthy. Monitor for pests like aphids regularly.
Tomato___Bacterial_spot: 1. Avoid overhead watering. 2. Remove infected lower leaves. 3. Apply copper-based bactericides every 7-10 days if severe. 4. Practice crop rotation.
Tomato___healthy: Your tomato leaf is healthy. Ensure adequate staking and air flow.
